steps.title: Use the historical contrast
steps.content: Modern audiences are used to carefully designed sets, so simple stages can surprise them now. But the text says decorated, detailed sets were not common until the 1600s.

steps.title: Reject surprise-based choices
steps.content: Choice B, Choice C, and Choice D all imply that simple stages were strange, exciting, confusing, or unwelcome to earlier audiences. That conflicts with the claim that such stages were historically normal.

steps.title: Choose the normal reaction
steps.content: Choice A fits because audiences in the distant past would have expected simple stages if highly decorated sets had not yet become common. The answer is A.
